talk_metadata.talk_title: Stromal microenvironment as a predictor of bone metastasis in prostate cancer
rhetorical_signals: One caveat is that clinical trials targeting the stromal component have not been very successful
we think very strongly that periostin is associated both with neuroendocrine differentiation and organotropism

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Neglect of fibroblast heterogeneity and AR‑loss driven periostin signaling

key_data_gaps: No clinical trial data on stromal‑targeting agents in prostate cancer
Lack of prospective validation of the periostin stromal signature in independent patient cohorts

crispro_opportunity.description: Periostin‑rich stromal signature could be used to stratify patients for bone‑metastasis risk and to select trials of bone‑targeted therapies.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: The speaker showed that a periostin‑based stromal signature predicts metastasis in an independent Mayo Clinic cohort.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate periostin‑related transcriptomic features with spatial data to generate a predictive risk model for bone tropism.

crispro_opportunity.description: Existing CAF‑targeting drugs that failed could be repurposed by pairing with agents that block periostin‑αVβ3/αVβ5 signaling.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that clinical trials targeting the stromal component have not been very successful, suggesting a need for new combinatorial approaches.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model combinatorial effects of periostin inhibition with other pathway inhibitors to prioritize salvage strategies.

cited_competitors.name: Oncotype DX
cited_competitors.context: The stromal gene signature overlaps with three genes used in the Oncotype DX assay.

unresolved_questions: What determines the differential periostin expression between liver and bone metastases?
Can periostin inhibition reverse neuroendocrine differentiation in vivo?

assets_to_track: CAF‑directed clinical trials in prostate cancer

talk_metadata.talk_title: Metabolic coding of organotropism in breast cancer metastasis
rhetorical_signals: Metastatic success is not about brute force. It is all the growth signaling nutrient requirements that are markedly different in the different metastatic niches
We were quite keen to show that bone metastatic cancer cells are very sensitive to glutamine dependency

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Redundant glutamine transporters limit efficacy of single transporter inhibition

key_data_gaps: No in‑human validation of GLS1 inhibitor efficacy in bone metastasis

crispro_opportunity.description: Targeting GLS1 activity (e.g., with CBA39) could overcome glutamine addiction of bone‑metastatic breast cancer cells.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker showed that bone metastatic cells have higher GLS1 activity and are sensitive to a GLS1 inhibitor in vitro.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict GLS1 dependency across patient transcriptomes and prioritize patients for GLS1‑targeted trials.

crispro_opportunity.description: A glutamine‑dependency transcriptional signature could stratify patients likely to develop bone metastases.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Integration of RNA‑seq and metabolomics identified a bone‑specific glutamine‑uptake program.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can generate a composite score linking glutamine transporter expression and GLS1 activity to clinical outcomes.

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine GLS1 inhibition with agents that block compensatory pathways (e.g., mTOR inhibitors) to prevent metabolic escape.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Bone metastatic cells rely on glutamine‑derived α‑ketoglutarate to sustain mTOR signaling.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model synergistic effects of dual metabolic inhibition using pathway interaction maps.

cited_competitors.name: Vander Heiden lab
cited_competitors.context: Provided example of fatty‑acid synthesis dependency in brain metastasis.

cited_competitors.name: Sarah Fenn lab
cited_competitors.context: Demonstrated pyruvate carboxylase dependency in lung metastasis.

unresolved_questions: What is the source of extracellular glutamine in the bone microenvironment?
Can targeting glutamine metabolism affect osteoblast function and bone health?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Circulating tumor cell heterogeneity and the role of TMPO in metastasis
rhetorical_signals: TMPO is a nuclear membrane‑associated protein that allows cells to survive stress in circulation
We think TMPO is allowing the cells to survive the stress and to find their home at the metastatic site

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of therapeutic agents that specifically inhibit TMPO‑driven hypoxia‑oxphos switch
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: TMPO is strikingly strongly expressed in a large percentage of circulating tumor cells from advanced prostate cancer patients

key_data_gaps: No in‑human functional validation of TMPO as a therapeutic target
Unclear whether TMPO inhibition affects normal hematopoietic or stromal cells

crispro_opportunity.description: TMPO expression in CTCs could serve as a prognostic biomarker for metastatic risk and bone tropism in prostate cancer.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker showed that TMPO knockdown reduces CTC numbers and bone metastasis in mouse models and that TMPO is highly expressed in patient CTCs.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate TMPO transcript levels with CTC enumeration data to generate a risk score for aggressive disease.

crispro_opportunity.description: Existing drugs that modulate nuclear lamina interactions could be repurposed to target TMPO‑driven pathways.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: TMPO is a Lamin‑Associated Protein 2 that drives a hypoxia‑oxphos program in CTCs.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can screen libraries of nuclear‑targeting compounds for predicted impact on TMPO‑regulated gene networks.

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine TMPO inhibition with GLS1 or oxidative‑phosphorylation modulators to block both hypoxic survival and metabolic flexibility of CTCs.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Knockdown of TMPO shifts CTC organoids from a hypoxic to an ox‑phos program, reducing metastatic capacity.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model synergistic effects of dual targeting on CTC survival pathways.

cited_competitors.name: Califano group
cited_competitors.context: Collaborated on master regulator analysis of CTC single‑cell data.

cited_competitors.name: Peter Sims
cited_competitors.context: Lead author on the CTC organoid paper.

unresolved_questions: Does TMPO expression differ between single CTCs and CTC clusters?
What upstream signals regulate TMPO activity during circulation?

assets_to_track: TMPO/LAP2 inhibitors (if any in preclinical pipelines)
CTC enumeration platforms for prostate cancer
companies_to_monitor: Companies developing nuclear envelope targeting therapeutics
